calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Continued To Nov-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-11-2018 Continued To Oct-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridman for and with (Executrix) Michele Marsh.

The Court stated that this is for a petition for final distribution, that there are a few questions and asked if notice was mailed to the Franchise Tax Board. Mr. Bridgman stated that returns were filed but notice was not mailed. The Court stated that this does not satisfy the statutory notice requirement. Mr. Bridgman stated that the taxes were paid a long time ago, that there have been many continuances and that they were never told about the issue of notice. The Court asked about the money market account. Mr. Bridgman responded that it consists of $112.00, that this amount is on the bank statements, that they did not consider it important and that they are trying to transfer real property into the trust. The Court stated that there are some assets with joint ownership but the owners are not being noticed. Mr. Bridgman replied that the Santoro account is her company, that Mr. David is the bookkeeper, that the company does not belong to him but is titled in both of their names so that he can write checks. The Court asked if Mr. David has ever received notice. Mr. Bridgman stated that Mr. David knows of the proceedings, has not received notice and that the bank accounts do not need to be included in this. The Court stated that if they are not included it will need an amended petition and that in addition there is no prayer for relief in the current petition. The petition for final distribution was continued to October 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for appropriate amendments or supplements to be filed and for notice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with petitioner Michelle Marsh.

Mr. Bridgman reported that one of the beneficiaries of the estate may have to be conserved, so he had been waiting for a determination of that question. He added that the other reason for delay had been the need to obtain a correct property description for one of the three time shares, which would then need to be submitted to the probate referee. He stated that he expected to have the information to the probate referee within a week. The Court expressed concern regarding the delay in filing of the required inventory and appraisal, and directed that an inventory and appraisal as well as a petition for approval of status of administration be filed within 30 days. The matter was continued to March 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear was taken off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-10-2018 Continued To Feb-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Executor and the Attorney to appear on February 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear, and why an inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
